This is tricky to get right I think, so I was cautious, as buffering doesn't provide much benefit here - we're expecting <100 of log lines for a unit test case.

The py2/py3 version makes it interesting too.

In py3, the file-like object I pass to Popen has to be in binary mode, and thus cannot be line buffered, only block buffered, which we don't really want.

In py2, it only supports block buffering anyway, AFAICS, or -1 for the operating system default (which is block buffered for stdout, right?)

So having no buffering for output, and using readline() for input, seemed the best from a correctness pov.
